prevalence.source: ICMR-informed hospital epidemiology + IHME GBD (VTE) context
prevalence.trend: increasing recognition with expanding CT availability in tertiary hospitals

incidence.source: IHME GBD (VTE) extrapolated; Indian tertiary-center registries

burden.mortalityRate: 5-20 per 100,000 (higher with delayed diagnosis and limited ICU access)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical probability scoring (Wells), D-dimer where available, confirmation by CTPA; lower-extremity ultrasound for DVT; Indian practice aligned with international/ESC approaches

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; inpatient prophylaxis implementation varies widely
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; affected by out-of-pocket costs for diagnostics and anticoagulants

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: CTPA mainly in tertiary centers; peripheral facilities rely on clinical suspicion and referral
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Anticoagulation with heparin/LMWH followed by DOACs or warfarin; thrombolysis for high-risk PE when available; hospital VTE prophylaxis protocols
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis, limited CTPA/ICU access outside metros, and adherence challenges due to cost and follow-up fragmentation
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing tertiary hospital capacity and increasing availability of affordable generic anticoagulants
